metadata.summary: Generate images from text prompts using DALL-E 3, Flux, or Stable Diffusion. Covers model selection, prompt engineering, and automated pipeline patterns.

content.title: AI Image Generation: Model Selection and Pipeline Automation
content.summary: AI image generation from text prompts using DALL-E 3, Flux (Black Forest Labs), Stable Diffusion, and related APIs. Covers model selection, prompt engineering formula ([Subject] + [Style] + [Lighting] + [Composition] + [Details] + [Technical]), API integration patterns, and pipeline automation for content at scale.

why.p: Each model has a distinct strength profile: DALL-E 3 and GPT-4o lead on text rendering and prompt adherence; Flux leads on photorealism and anatomy; Stable Diffusion + LoRA is the only option for brand-specific fine-tuned assets; Flux schnell (Apache 2.0) is the cost-effective choice for high-volume batch generation. Selecting the wrong model wastes cost or produces unusable output.

list.item: Generating marketing visuals, social media graphics, or product mockups as part of an automated content pipeline
Producing custom illustrations for articles, blog posts, or newsletters where stock photography is inadequate
Rapid UI/UX prototyping: visualizing interface concepts before implementation
Generating variation sets (A/B test creatives) at scale for advertising campaigns
Brand asset generation with consistent style through fine-tuned models or style references
AI news pipelines where each article requires a unique header image generated from the article summary
Legal/medical/financial documents where image hallucinations create liability
Photorealistic images of real named people — content policy violations plus legal risk (likeness rights)
Logo design requiring precise vector output — generative models produce raster; use a designer or vector tools
High-volume generation where per-image cost matters at scale — self-host Flux schnell (Apache 2.0) instead of paying per API call
Consistent character generation across many images without Flux Kontext or LoRA — models don't preserve identity by default
Anything requiring exact pixel-level control (infographics with precise data, technical diagrams)

section.name: model-selection
section.p: Different image generation models excel at different tasks. Understanding their strengths and limitations is critical for cost efficiency and output quality.

rule.statement: Use DALL-E 3 or GPT-4o when the image must contain readable text; no other model reliably renders strings under 25 characters.
rule.rationale: Text rendering is unreliable across all generative models for strings longer than 25 characters. DALL-E 3 and GPT-4o have the highest text accuracy but still fail on longer strings.

rule.statement: Use Flux schnell (Apache 2.0, self-hosted) for high-volume bulk generation where per-API-call cost matters; use Flux Dev or Pro for quality-critical photorealistic output.
rule.rationale: At scale, $0.014+ per image compounds quickly. Flux schnell is Apache 2.0 licensed, runs in 1-4 steps, and is suitable for social media post images and bulk pipeline output.

rule.statement: Use Stable Diffusion + LoRA for brand-specific assets requiring consistent visual identity that cannot be achieved through prompt keywords alone.
rule.rationale: Standard API models produce visually different results on identical prompts. Only fine-tuned models (LoRA) or reference-image workflows (Flux Kontext) preserve brand identity reliably.

section.p: Model profiles: DALL-E 3 — best prompt adherence, text rendering, structured JSON response. Flux Dev/Pro — best photorealism, anatomy, character consistency. Flux schnell — fastest (1-4 steps), Apache 2.0 for self-hosting. Midjourney — most artistic; no official API, not suitable for automation.

section.name: prompt-engineering
section.p: Structured prompt engineering produces consistent, high-quality output across regenerations and models.

rule.statement: Always use the structured prompt formula: [Subject] + [Style] + [Lighting] + [Composition] + [Details] + [Technical]; vague prompts produce inconsistent results across regenerations.
rule.rationale: Each dimension controls a different visual aspect. Missing lighting or composition often produces flat, uninteresting images that require expensive retry cycles.

example.title: Structured prompt following the 6-element formula
example.code: A futuristic city skyline at sunset, digital art style with vibrant colors, golden hour lighting with warm tones, wide angle composition, flying cars and glass buildings, 8K resolution, cinematic film grain

section.name: pipeline-and-automation
section.p: Production image generation requires a multi-stage pipeline to handle prompt quality, model selection, and output validation.

rule.statement: Use a three-stage pipeline: prompt-engineer subagent transforms brief → image-generator selects model and calls API → quality-checker VLM verifies output matches requirements.
rule.rationale: Separating concerns prevents the generator from making prompt quality decisions and the prompt engineer from handling API errors. Each stage has a clear, testable responsibility.

section.p: Quality-checker uses a vision model (Claude or GPT-4o) to verify subject present, style correct, no obvious artifacts. On failure, loop retries with revised prompt up to 3 times before escalating.

rule.statement: Pre-filter prompts through a content-policy checker before hitting the API; rejected requests still consume latency and may count against rate limits.
rule.rationale: Content policy rejections are unpredictable on borderline prompts. Pre-filtering with a lightweight classifier reduces API 400 errors and improves pipeline throughput.

section.name: cost-model
section.p: Image generation costs vary significantly by model and quality level. Planning for scale requires understanding the cost structure.
DALL-E 3: $0.04-0.08/image (standard), $0.08-0.12/image (HD). Flux Pro via API: $0.014+/image. Flux schnell self-hosted: GPU rental cost only. Midjourney: $10-60/month subscription.

rule.statement: Cache all generated images immediately using prompt+parameters hash as the cache key; identical prompts sent twice waste money and API rate limits.
rule.rationale: Image generation APIs are expensive relative to text. Regenerating the same image in a pipeline retry loop compounds costs without benefit.

section.name: failure-modes-and-gotchas
section.p: Common mistakes in image generation pipelines can waste significant budget or produce unusable output.

antipattern.description: Text rendering in images is unreliable for strings longer than 25 characters even with GPT-4o/DALL-E 3.
antipattern.reason: Never use image generation for text-heavy content. If text is required, use DALL-E 3 and keep text under 25 characters.

antipattern.description: Consistent character appearance across a series requires seeds (SD) or reference image support (Flux Kontext).
antipattern.reason: Standard API calls produce visually different people each time. Plan for this in character-driven content pipelines.

antipattern.description: DALL-E 3's `revised_prompt` silently modifies your prompt for safety.
antipattern.reason: The generated image may not match the input prompt; log `revised_prompt` for debugging and base quality checks on the actual revised prompt.

antipattern.description: Content policy rejections are unpredictable on borderline prompts.
antipattern.reason: Implement retry with softened prompt and exponential backoff.

antipattern.description: Aspect ratio must match intended use case at generation time.
antipattern.reason: Upscaling or cropping after the fact degrades quality significantly.

antipattern.description: Storing OpenAI image URLs without immediately downloading the image.
antipattern.reason: Image URLs from the OpenAI API expire after 1 hour. An agent that logs the URL for later retrieval will receive a 404 when the pipeline processes it.

antipattern.description: Asserting the generated image matches the input prompt without checking `revised_prompt`.
antipattern.reason: DALL-E 3 silently modifies prompts for safety. The `revised_prompt` field shows what was actually generated; quality checks must be based on this, not the original prompt.

antipattern.description: Submitting batch generation jobs without rate limit throttling.
antipattern.reason: DALL-E 3 is rate-limited to 5 images/minute on Tier 1. Agent batch jobs without throttling exhaust the rate limit in the first batch and stall all subsequent requests.

list.item: Always cache generated images — identical prompts sent twice waste money; hash the prompt + parameters as cache key
Log `revised_prompt` from DALL-E 3 alongside the output — it shows what the model actually generated vs. what you requested; essential for debugging drift
Use negative prompts in Stable Diffusion (`blurry, deformed, ugly, watermark`) to filter common artifacts; negative prompts don't exist in DALL-E API
For consistent brand style, use Flux Kontext with a reference image rather than trying to engineer style consistency through prompt keywords alone
Match the generation model's strength to the task: `dall-e-3` for editorial/conceptual, `flux-1-schnell` for bulk social posts, `sdxl + LoRA` for brand-specific assets
For regulated industries (HIPAA/SOC2), use AWS Bedrock + Stability AI SD 3.5 Enterprise
Human approval checkpoint: for paid advertising channels, require human review before publishing — automated quality checks miss brand guideline violations

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: A structured workflow for integrating image generation into multi-agent systems:

rule.statement: Use a prompt-engineer subagent to transform a raw content brief into a structured image prompt using the formula [Subject] + [Style] + [Lighting] + [Composition] + [Details] + [Technical], then pass the structured prompt to an image-generator subagent.
rule.rationale: This separation of concerns ensures high-quality prompts and proper error handling.

rule.statement: The image-generator subagent selects the model based on requirements (artistic → Midjourney-style prompts for Flux Dev; OCR text in image → DALL-E 3/GPT-4o; high-volume batch → Flux schnell self-hosted) and calls the API.
rule.rationale: Model selection must be automated based on task requirements, not guessed.

rule.statement: A quality-checker subagent uses a vision model to verify the generated image matches key requirements before accepting it; on failure, the loop retries with a revised prompt up to 3 times.
rule.rationale: Automated quality checking catches obvious failures early without human intervention, but should not replace human review for high-stakes content.

list.item: Query classifier subagent — detects if query contains exact terms (codes, quoted phrases, names) and returns {type: "semantic"|"keyword"|"hybrid", alpha: float}
Batch-scheduler subagent — manages concurrent generation requests within rate limits; queues and retries

template.purpose: Prompt to transform a content brief into a structured generation prompt
DALL-E 3 batch generation with content-policy retry and URL expiry handling
